item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Top Iranian Official Is Believed Killed, Israel Says
item.description: Ali Larijani, one of Iran’s most senior leaders, was killed in overnight strikes, Israel’s defense minister said. Iran did not immediately comment on the claim.

item.title: China is not going to bail Trump out
item.description: The US president has a better chance of cajoling help from Nato partners

item.title: At Least 23 Killed in Nigeria as Insurgent Attacks Go On Despite U.S. Help
item.description: A teaching hospital and two markets in the city of Maiduguri were hit in what a military spokesman said were suicide bombings by Boko Haram.

item.title: We Traced How U.S. Guns Get to Mexican Cartels
item.description: Gun traffickers have quietly moved what they say is an unprecedented number of weapons from the United States to Mexico. Our reporter Paulina Villegas followed the trail of these guns.

item.title: Investors pile into cash on fears of Iran war fallout
item.description: Markets left with ‘few places to hide’ from disruption caused by conflict

item.title: Hundreds Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul, Officials in Afghanistan Say
item.description: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, Afghanistan said, suggesting that its victims included civilians. Pakistan said it had targeted an ammunitions depot.

item.title: Easter holidaymakers switching from Dubai to Spain as flights fill up
item.description: It comes after the war in Iran caused mass disruption to flights across the Middle East and UAE.

item.title: Israel says it has killed Iran’s top security official
item.description: Veteran conservative Ali Larijani was one of regime’s key leaders after assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

item.title: Pakistan air strike kills at least 100 at Kabul drug rehab centre
item.description: Some of the bodies were injured beyond recognition, forensic laboratory sources told the BBC.

item.title: Ukraine makes rare advance inside ‘kill zone’
item.description: Kyiv touts battlefield success as proof it is still in the fight as Russia gears up for ‘summer offensive’

item.title: What to Know About Ali Larijani, Iran’s Top Security Official
item.description: One of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s closest confidants, Mr. Larijani emerged as the face of the government after Mr. Khamenei was killed.

item.title: Trio of European states set up new fund to boost defence spending
item.description: UK, Netherlands and Finland join forces to reduce procurement costs through economies of scale

item.title: Trump seeks to delay meeting with Xi in China
item.description: The escalating war in Iran has overshadowed many of the US' foreign policy objectives

item.title: Millions without electricity as Cuba's power grid collapses
item.description: Cuba's chronic fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US blockade on oil shipments to the island.

item.title: Europe Has Helped the U.S. Protect Shipping. The Strait of Hormuz Is Different.
item.description: European Union ministers and leaders pushed back on ideas to protect the strait, including expanding a maritime operation already in place in the Red Sea.

item.title: Suspected suicide attacks kill at least 23 in north-east Nigeria
item.description: Multiple blasts struck some of the Maiduguri's busiest locations on Monday evening.

item.title: FirstFT: European allies reject Trump’s Hormuz armada demand
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: top hedge fund says problems in private credit worse than Wall Street admits and Jensen Huang’s bullish outlook

item.title: UN receives reports alleging torture of detainees in Venezuela continues
item.description: The UN is deeply concerned by allegations there have been incidents of torture after Nicolás Maduro's ouster.

item.title: Warner Bros chief David Zaslav in line for $700mn payday
item.description: One of Hollywood’s highest earners stands to reap huge rewards from Paramount’s $111bn takeover of Warner Bros Discovery

item.title: Cuba Ready to Accept Outside Investment, Top Official Says
item.description: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

item.title: Oil climbs as Iran steps up attacks across Gulf
item.description: Strikes come as Israel says it killed Iran’s top security official

item.title: Another Tanker Is Attacked Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The ship was struck near an Emirati port, in the first such attack in five days. The United Arab Emirates also said it was intercepting Iranian drones and missiles.

item.title: Behind Badenoch’s theory that Starmer is courting ‘sectarian’ votes
item.description: Tory leader’s misreading of UK public opposition to Tehran stems from her worldview of a ‘clash of civilisations’

item.title: China is Tight-Lipped on Trump’s Request to Delay His Xi Summit
item.description: After President Trump said he needed to postpone his meeting in Beijing with Xi Jinping, a spokesman for China said officials were still discussing the timing of a meeting.

item.title: Dubai flights delayed or cancelled after latest drone and missile attacks
item.description: Regional travel hubs Doha and Abu Dhabi also severely constrained by fallout from conflict

item.title: As Carney Seeks New Alliances for Canada, He Looks Away on Human Rights
item.description: Canada’s prime minister chooses pragmatism in a turbulent world, which means doing business with countries that do not share Canada’s democratic values. Some critics see this as weakness.

item.title: Sucked Into War, Gulf Countries Face the Limits of U.S. Security Guarantees
item.description: Barraged by Iranian attacks and questioning the value of security ties with the United States, nations in the Gulf have turned to Ukraine, Australia and Italy for help.

item.title: Inside the Supply Line Delivering American Guns to Mexican Cartels
item.description: A surge of weapons is flowing from the U.S. to Mexico. These firearms — sourced from gun shops, shows, websites and apps — are funneled across the border to fuel the country’s most violent crimes.

item.title: Zelensky to visit Starmer to sign new Ukraine-UK defence pact
item.description: Downing Street says the agreement bring together "Ukrainian expertise and the UK's industrial base".

item.title: Trump 'not happy' with UK response to Iran conflict
item.description: It comes after Sir Keir Starmer said the UK was working with allies on a plan to protect the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: This is what happened on March 16.
In Iraq, the U.S. Tried to Bring Allies on Board. Not in Iran.
item.description: This time, President Trump went to war without preparing the public, seeking U.N. approval or even consulting allies. But they will have to pick up the pieces.

item.title: Quantum computing and AI to get £2.5bn to stop UK tech 'drifting abroad', Reeves vows
item.description: The chancellor tells the BBC she wants the "pattern to end" while also pledging closer ties with the EU.

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang predicts $1tn in AI chip revenue over 2 years
item.description: Chip group’s higher than expected sales forecast fails to boost share price

item.title: Gas on the line: will the Iran war squeeze India's piped gas next?
item.description: The Iran war has already rattled LPG users. Will India’s piped gas network face the next squeeze?

item.title: BNP bets European private credit boom can defy US downturn
item.description: ​Asset management chief points to continent’s financing needs and more stringent regulation to protect from mis-selling

item.title: Oil producers: buy the peace, not the war
item.description: Plus, PCE data drama

item.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
item.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.

item.title: Arsenal trial and self-doubt: Valverde's journey to Real legend
item.description: Rejected by Arsenal as a teenager, Federico Valverde has transformed himself into a Real Madrid legend - and was the main man in their Champions League win over Manchester City last week.

item.title: Young Venezuelans voice hope and frustration as post-Maduro future unfolds
item.description: Young people who have only known one political system are adjusting to change after US forces seized Maduro.

item.title: Reform pledges to scrap VAT and green levies on energy bills
item.description: There are fears the war in Iran could lead to a surge in household energy bills.

item.title: Europe rules out joining Trump’s Hormuz armada
item.description: Also in this newsletter: The Iran war’s cost to combustion engine cars

item.title: Who ate all the Chinese stock market returns?
item.description: China got rich. Less so equity punters.

item.title: Hundreds feared dead in strike on Kabul hospital
item.description: Taliban blames Pakistan for attack that threatens to escalate neighbours’ conflict

item.title: Australia raises interest rates in big week for global central banks
item.description: Oil disruption deepens concern that price increases could hit economy

item.title: Why did only two Iranian football players stay in Australia?
item.description: After Australia gave humanitarian visas to seven team members, only two decided to stay - the BBC's Katy Watson explains what happened.

item.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
item.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

item.title: The golden age of private credit (distress)
item.description: Credit hedge fund Davidson Kempner warns the sector’s problems are deeper than Wall Street has acknowledged

item.title: Who is winning the Middle East war?
item.description: Iran has taken a beating but it retains the advantages of geography, time and a superior tolerance for pain

item.title: Naval escorts will not guarantee safe passage through Strait of Hormuz, says IMO chief
item.description: Arsenio Dominguez does not believe military protection for ships is a sustainable solution

item.title: Sri Lanka declares Wednesdays off as Asian countries try to conserve fuel
item.description: This is the latest in a series of measures undertaken by Asian countries which rely on oil from the Gulf.

item.title: As American Bombers Take Off From R.A.F. Fairford in UK, Iraq War Looms Large
item.description: R.A.F. Fairford was the site of repeated antiwar protests during the Iraq war in 2003. Now it is being used again as a base for U.S. bombing missions in the Middle East.

item.title: Chris Mason: Why Starmer thinks he's called it right on war despite Trump barbs
item.description: The BBC's Political Editor Chris Mason considers the US president's recent jabs at the UK prime minister.

item.title: India's outsourcing industry is worth $300bn. Can it survive AI?
item.description: Indian IT stocks have plunged as fears grow of AI disrupting back offices. But some say these are overblown.

item.title: Intrigue, Power Plays and Rivalries: Inside the Rise of Mojtaba Khamenei
item.description: The weeklong fight over Iran’s next leader pitted the Revolutionary Guards against moderates. The generals won, but only over spirited resistance.

item.title: Surge in US petrol prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
item.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

item.title: 'Fire came from the sky and burned them' - life on the brink of civil war in South Sudan
item.description: Some 50,000 people are told to flee their homes as the army warns of a huge military offensive in Jonglei state.

item.title: Starmer announces £53m support to help with heating oil costs
item.description: The money will be for "vulnerable" households who have faced a sharp rise in energy bills since the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: The quiet recovery of Ireland’s ancient tongue
item.description: In the land of St Patrick, Irish is making a steady comeback

item.title: How the Makers of ‘Mr. Nobody Against Putin’ Addressed ‘an Ethical Minefield’
item.description: The documentary about Vladimir V. Putin’s wartime indoctrination of Russian schoolchildren won an Oscar at Sunday’s Academy Awards.

item.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
item.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

item.title: Fifa sanctions Nigeria and DR Congo over World Cup play-off
item.description: Fifa fines the Nigeria Football Federation and DR Congo Football Association for separate disciplinary breaches by supporters.

item.title: Starmer warned over assisted dying bill by more than 100 Labour MPs
item.description: More than 100 Labour MPs warn that failing to pass the legislation would undermine trust in politics.

item.title: 'My mother cried out one last time': Palestinian boy, 12, describes how Israeli forces killed his family in car
item.description: Khaled Bani Odeh's parents and two brothers were shot dead as they drove home from a shopping trip in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
item.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

item.title: Will America’s Asian allies get dragged into the Iran war?
item.description: They are worried Uncle Sam will abandon them if they don’t fight

item.title: Mexico City breaks Guinness Record with giant football training session
item.description: Mexico City set a Guinness record for the largest football training session as 9,500 participants filled the capital’s Zocalo square ahead of World Cup.

item.title: The War Room newsletter: A conflict Trump was ill-prepared for
item.description: There is no simple way to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, writes Shashank Joshi, our defence editor

item.title: A Maoist survival guide to the Iranian energy crisis
item.description: For a self-reliant power like China, no supplier is ever irreplaceable

item.title: Labour calls for tax investigation into Reform's Tice
item.description: It follows a newspaper report that Tice had avoided corporation tax worth hundreds of thousands of pounds.

item.title: Russia agrees to stop using Kenyan recruits in Ukraine conflict, Kenya says
item.description: Some 1,000 Kenyans are believed to have recruited to fight in Ukraine - many say they were lured under false pretences.

item.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
item.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

item.title: Companies offered £3,000 to hire jobless under-24s
item.description: It comes as ministers grapple with spiralling unemployment rates among young people.

item.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
item.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

item.title: What Do Africans Think of Trump’s Travel Ban List?
item.description: The restrictions on half the continent have been called racist and unfair. “We don’t come to the United States because we’re running away,” one N.B.A. fan said.

item.title: Whistleblower murder suspect is former elite officer, South African police say
item.description: Marius van der Merwe was shot dead three weeks after accusing police officers of killing a robbery suspect.

item.title: Ecuador deploys 75,000 soldiers and police to combat drug gangs
item.description: Citizens of the most violent-wracked provinces have been warned the government is "at war" with the gangs.

item.title: French national jailed for 10 years in Azerbaijan for spying
item.description: Martin Ryan was accused of collecting secret information about Baku's military co-operation with Turkey and Pakistan.

item.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
item.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

item.title: Far-left and far-right gains throw French mainstream parties into a quandary
item.description: Awkward choices emerge for mainstream parties after the first round of mayoral elections.

item.title: Key Oscars moments as snubbed Chalamet becomes butt of jokes
item.description: Here's what happened inside the winners room and other insights from the biggest night in Hollywood.

item.title: Is Cheburashka, the Beloved Soviet-Born Character, Ruining Russia?
item.description: Instead of obsessing over the fictional Cheburashka, Russians should be focused on more important things like the rebirth of a Russian empire, influential conservatives say.

item.title: Kpop Demon Hunters wins Oscars for best animated film and best original song
item.description: The Oscars are the latest in a string of accolades for the film, which include Golden Globe and Grammy awards.

item.title: India hopeful Iran talks will ease Hormuz route for its ships
item.description: Foreign Minister S Jaishankar told FT there was no "blanket arrangement" for Indian ships to pass through the strait.

item.title: Laptop found in search for missing Brazilian woman
item.description: Police also believe a boat Vitoria Barreto may have taken is missing a buoyancy aid.

item.title: Scotland's assisted dying debate reaches final stages on eve of vote
item.description: The long-running political battle over assisted dying comes to an end as MSPs get ready to vote.

item.title: Meta and TikTok let harmful content rise after evidence outrage drove engagement, say whistleblowers
item.description: Companies allowed more harmful content on user’s feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage, BBC hears.

item.title: Ten killed in fire at India hospital intensive care unit
item.description: All the victims of the fire in Odisha state are patients, and 11 hospital staff are being treated for burns.

item.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
item.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

item.title: How did Oscar winner Kpop Demon Hunters win hearts the world over?
item.description: We find out how the award-winning, demon-busting crooners have cast a spell world over.

item.title: One-off £2,000 grant gives care leavers head start, study finds
item.description: Participants were less likely to become homeless or spend time in hospital or prison, researchers say.

item.title: Fears of two-tier health system as more turn to private care, says watchdog
item.description: The patient watchdog warns of two-tier service as polling shows numbers paying for care is on the rise.

item.title: Is this product 'human-made'? The race to establish an AI-free logo
item.description: The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

item.title: Ukraine's urgent fight on the financial frontline
item.description: The war-torn country is battling to secure crucial funding from the IMF and EU, as well as putting up taxes.

item.title: 'We will go wherever they hide': Rooting out IS in Somalia
item.description: Somalia became a key part of IS's global network after fighters were driven out of strongholds in Syria and Iraq.

item.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
item.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

item.title: Sinner matches Federer & Djokovic with Indian Wells win
item.description: Jannik Sinner said it "means a lot to me" after he becomes the youngest man to complete the set of hard-court trophies with victory over Daniil Medvedev in the Indian Wells final.

item.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
item.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

item.title: Ugandan opposition leader tells BBC he fled abroad fearing for his life
item.description: Bobi Wine says from an undisclosed location: "It was clear that the regime wanted to eliminate me."

item.title: Why the Iran crisis caught Europe flat-footed
item.description: The EU suffers the war’s fallout, but can do little to cut it short

item.title: The Iran war may be about to escalate
item.description: Gulf states could join the conflict

item.title: Open-source intelligence shuts down
item.description: Satellite images of the Middle East are suddenly disappearing

item.title: What Nitish Kumar did for Bihar, India’s poorest state
item.description: The state’s longest-serving chief minister raised voters’ expectations of their politicians

item.title: Spain-Argentina Finalissima in Qatar cancelled
item.description: The Finalissima between Argentina and Spain set to be held in Qatar this month is cancelled because of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: UK must build own nuclear missiles to end reliance on US, say Lib Dems
item.description: Sir Ed Davey is calling on the government to start work on a "fully independent British nuclear deterrent".

item.title: Zelensky accuses EU allies of 'blackmail' in oil pipeline row
item.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says restoring the flow of Russian oil via Ukraine into the EU would be like lifting sanctions on Russia.

item.title: We will intervene on energy bills if necessary, says Miliband
item.description: Oil and gas prices have surged due to the US-Israel war in Iran, with fears over the cost of living.

item.title: Police-style powers to tackle fly-tippers being considered
item.description: The move would allow officers to search premises without a warrant, seize assets and arrest those suspected of criminality.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian couple and two of their children in occupied West Bank
item.description: Mohammed, aged five, and Othman, aged seven, were killed alongside their parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

item.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
item.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

item.title: Caddy faces 'hardest weeks' on New Zealand trek
item.description: Dougie Haynes, originally from Oxford, says the hardest section of his trek comes with "spectacular views".

item.title: An eccentric new friend entered her life. Then things took a strange, expensive turn
item.description: What began as a dog-sitting favour ended with boutique owner Bridget Hutchcroft being owed more than £1.6m.

item.title: Will Trump help or hinder Zimbabwe's white farmers in their compensation battle?
item.description: They have engaged a lobbying firm with close ties to the US president to argue their case.

item.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
item.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

item.title: German philosopher and social critic Jürgen Habermas dies at 96
item.description: One the most influential thinkers in post-war Germany, he linked philosophy and political action throughout his life.

item.title: Rescuers blame weather and 'underprepared skiers' for rise in Alps avalanche deaths
item.description: The BBC joins a French Alps rescue team as the number of skiers killed this season passes 100.

item.title: Former Labour minister dies after cancer diagnosis
item.description: The ex-Oldham MP Phil Woolas battled brain cancer for more than a year, his family says.

item.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
item.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
item.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

item.title: More US Marines and warships being moved to Middle East, reports say
item.description: The reinforcements will come from an amphibious ready group that is usually based in Japan, an official told CBS, the BBC's US partner.

item.title: Explosion at Amsterdam Jewish school 'a deliberate attack' says mayor
item.description: Security had been increased at Jewish public institutions following an earlier incident in Rotterdam.
